original_abstract: This research assesses the capacity of Large Language Models (LLMs) to represent geometric structures and their respective spatial relations. Utilizing BERT and GPT-2, we encoded geometries provided in Well-Known Text (WKT) format and processed the resulting embeddings through downstream classifiers and regressors to evaluate how well these models capture geometric attributes. Our experiments reveal that while LLM-generated embeddings effectively preserve geometry types and identify certain spatial relations (achieving up to 73% accuracy), they struggle significantly with precise numeric estimations, such as area and centroid calculations, and the retrieval of spatially related objects. The findings suggest that current foundation models lose critical quantitative nuance during tokenization. This research underscores the necessity of integrating domain-specific geospatial knowledge and developing more robust encoding strategies to support complex GeoAI applications.
humanized_abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly widely used, and it is natural to wonder whether they can affordably represent some basic elements of geometry with their underlying spatial relationships. Using BERT and GPT-2, we studied representations of the shapes described in Well-Known Text (WKT) format as inputs to the subsequent classification and regression tasks to see how well LLMs describe the inherent properties of these shapes. Our experiments show that even though LLM-based shape representations are surprisingly good at describing shapes as defined by their type and identifying their spatial relationships (up to 73% accuracy), they severely deteriorate any quantitative values that might exist between tokenization and therefore suffer from imprecision when it comes to calculating area or centroids and identifying neighbouring shapes. This study reveals that there is a strong need to (i) incorporate domain-specific knowledge into foundation models and (ii) develop new tokenization techniques for affording the required GeoAI use cases with high precision.
